$comment: 13 launch guides. Slugs+titles final (zod: title<=70). Each guide links its tools in prose; the paired tool pages link back from 'How it works'. Written in Phase D per docs/voice.md, 800-1500 words, one idea taught properly: background -> mechanism -> worked example -> practical advice.

guides.title: How Password Entropy Actually Works
guides.outline: What entropy measures (process, not string); log2 arithmetic with a table of pool sizes vs length; why 'Tr0ub4dor' style loses to length; offline vs online attack rates; realistic GPU numbers; common myths (complexity rules).

guides.title: Diceware Passphrases: Four Random Words Beat P@ssw0rd1
guides.outline: The xkcd 936 argument done rigorously; EFF large wordlist mechanics (7776 words, 12.9 bits/word); dice-roll provenance; when passphrases beat random strings (human memory) and when they don't; realistic length advice.

guides.title: What EXIF Data Reveals About You — and How to Strip It
guides.outline: What cameras/phones embed (GPS, serials, timestamps, software); real de-anonymisation cases (described generically, no names); which platforms strip EXIF on upload and which don't; how re-encoding removes it; limits (recompression).

guides.title: JWT Security Mistakes Developers Still Make
guides.outline: Decoding != verification; alg:none and algorithm-confusion attacks (HS/RS); putting secrets in payloads; long expiry + no revocation; localStorage vs httpOnly cookie trade-offs; sensible defaults checklist.

guides.title: How a Password Leak Check Works Without Seeing Your Password
guides.outline: The k-anonymity range protocol step by step with a worked SHA-1 example; why 5 hex chars ~ 500-1000 candidates; what HIBP can and cannot infer; why this beats naive 'send the hash'; response padding.

guides.title: Browser Fingerprinting Explained: Tracking Without Cookies
guides.outline: Entropy budget of common signals (UA, screen, fonts, canvas, WebGL); how bits combine to uniqueness; why clearing cookies doesn't help; what actually mitigates (Tor Browser letterboxing, Firefox RFP) and what's snake oil; honest advice.

guides.title: A Practical Guide to Cron Expressions
guides.outline: The five fields with a reference table; step/range/list syntax; DOM-vs-DOW OR gotcha; timezone and DST pitfalls; a dozen worked examples from real ops life; links to the /cron/ preset pages.

guides.title: Base64 Is Not Encryption: Encoding vs Encryption vs Hashing
guides.outline: The three concepts with one-line definitions and a comparison table; why base64 exists (binary-safe transport); the 4/3 size math; real incidents of base64-as-'security' (generic); when each is the right tool.

guides.title: How Password Managers Work — and How to Choose One
guides.outline: Vault encryption architecture (KDF from master password, zero-knowledge); what a breach of the vendor can and can't expose (Argon2/PBKDF2 iterations); autofill phishing protection; choosing criteria (audits, open source, platform fit); honest mentions incl. Bitwarden/KeePass free options alongside affiliate picks.

guides.title: What utm_, fbclid and gclid Actually Do
guides.outline: Param families table (analytics campaign tags vs click identifiers vs session leakage); how click IDs tie ad clicks to profiles; why stripping sometimes breaks links (signed params); browser features (Firefox query stripping) and their limits.

guides.title: What Your IP Address Reveals — and What a VPN Changes
guides.outline: What IP geolocation actually resolves (city-ish, ISP, not your house); who sees your IP (sites, ISP, DNS); what a VPN moves vs what it doesn't (fingerprinting, logins); honest 'do you need one' decision guide; mention Mullvad editorially (no affiliate) alongside affiliate picks — credibility matters more than one click.

guides.title: Does Your Website Need a Privacy Policy?
keywords: do i need a privacy policy
guides.outline: When laws apply (GDPR/UK, CCPA thresholds, Australian Privacy Act small-business nuance); what analytics/ads/forms trigger; minimum contents; generator-vs-lawyer honesty; NOT LEGAL ADVICE disclaimer prominent; keep sober — YMYL page.

guides.title: Why Math.random() Can't Make Passwords: CSPRNGs Explained
guides.outline: PRNG vs CSPRNG; xorshift128+ predictability (state recovery from outputs — describe, don't tutorialise); where browsers get entropy; modulo bias with a tiny worked example and rejection sampling; how this site's generators apply it.

guides.title: PIN Security: What Four Digits Can and Can't Do
guides.outline: 13.3 bits reality; why PINs work anyway (lockout + secure elements); common-PIN research (1234, dates — cite the well-known DataGenetics-style findings generically); when to use 6+ digits; device vs remote contexts.
